task_completion.description: Agent navigates to the Mirny Diamond Mine location.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The search bar contains 'Mirny', 'Mir mine', or coordinates near '62.5294, 113.9953', or the map view shows the distinct open-pit mine.

task_completion.description: The Mirny Diamond Mine is clearly visible and centered in the viewport.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A large, deep, spiral open-pit mine is visible, located immediately adjacent to a city/town infrastructure.

task_completion.description: Agent activates the Ruler or Measurement tool in the application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Ruler/Measure tool icon is highlighted, or a measurement dialog box is open.

task_completion.description: Agent draws a measurement line across the diameter of the pit.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A line is drawn spanning the width of the open pit from one rim to the opposite rim.

task_completion.description: The measured distance is within the acceptable range (1100m - 1300m).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The distance label associated with the line reads between 1,100 m (1.1 km) and 1,300 m (1.3 km).

task_completion.description: Agent saves the screenshot to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog shows the filename 'mirny_measurement.png' or a terminal command (e.g., scrot, gnome-screenshot) is executed with the path '~/mirny_measurement.png'.

integrity.description: Agent interacts with a mapping application GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a mapping interface (e.g., Marble, Google Earth, Maps) with visible controls, not just a static image loaded in an image viewer.

integrity.description: Agent does not download a pre-existing screenshot.
integrity.visual_evidence: No use of terminal commands like `wget` or `curl` to download image files from external URLs.

integrity.description: The measurement is performed dynamically, not hardcoded.
integrity.visual_evidence: The measurement line or points appear sequentially or the tool is manipulated via cursor, rather than the result instantly appearing via script.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The Mirny Diamond Mine is a massive open-pit mine located at approximately 62.52583°N, 113.98417°E. It is visually distinct as a giant spiral hole in the ground next to a city. The nominal diameter of the pit is 1,200 meters. Valid measurements for the rim-to-rim diameter should fall between 1,100 meters and 1,300 meters (1.1 km - 1.3 km).
